sub_criteria: Clarity and pertinence of the project's objectives, and the extent to which the proposed work is ambitious, and goes beyond the state of the art.

Soundness of the proposed methodology, including the underlying concepts, models, assumptions, inter-disciplinary approaches, appropriate consideration of the gender dimension in research and innovation content, and the quality of open science practices, including sharing and management of research outputs and engagement of citizens, civil society and end users where appropriate.

grade_descriptors.0: The proposal fails to address the criterion or cannot be assessed due to missing or incomplete information.
grade_descriptors.1: Poor. The criterion is inadequately addressed, or there are serious inherent weaknesses.
grade_descriptors.2: Fair. The proposal broadly addresses the criterion, but there are significant weaknesses.
grade_descriptors.3: Good. The proposal addresses the criterion well, but a number of shortcomings are present.
grade_descriptors.4: Very Good. The proposal addresses the criterion very well, but a small number of shortcomings are present.
grade_descriptors.5: Excellent. The proposal successfully addresses all relevant aspects of the criterion. Any shortcomings are minor.

Excellence.call_specific_priority_notes: Standard RIA evaluation criterion. Call topic emphasises: (1) significant advances in AI agent autonomy, robustness, and reliability; (2) innovative multi-agent frameworks and protocols; (3) mandatory incorporation of qualitative and quantitative KPIs, benchmarking, and progress monitoring mechanisms. Source: evaluation_priority_weights.json, criterion_id Excellence, priority_notes field.

sub_criteria: Credibility of the pathways to achieve the expected outcomes and impacts specified in the work programme.
Suitability and quality of the measures to maximise expected outcomes and impacts, as set out in the dissemination and exploitation plan, including communication activities.

grade_descriptors.0: The proposal fails to address the criterion or cannot be assessed due to missing or incomplete information.
grade_descriptors.1: Poor. The criterion is inadequately addressed, or there are serious inherent weaknesses.
grade_descriptors.2: Fair. The proposal broadly addresses the criterion, but there are significant weaknesses.
grade_descriptors.3: Good. The proposal addresses the criterion well, but a number of shortcomings are present.
grade_descriptors.4: Very Good. The proposal addresses the criterion very well, but a small number of shortcomings are present.
grade_descriptors.5: Excellent. The proposal successfully addresses all relevant aspects of the criterion. Any shortcomings are minor.

Impact.call_specific_priority_notes: Standard RIA evaluation criterion. Call-specific impact priorities include: contribution to the Apply AI Strategy; deployment via European Digital Innovation Hubs (EDIHs); mandatory cohesion with ADRA; portfolio coverage across healthcare, advanced manufacturing, and in-vehicle autonomous driving sectors. Portfolio award procedure explicitly adjusts ranking to ensure at least one project per sector passes all evaluation thresholds. Source: evaluation_priority_weights.json, criterion_id Impact, priority_notes field.

Quality and Efficiency of Implementation.criterion_name: Quality and efficiency of the implementation
sub_criteria: Quality and effectiveness of the work plan, assessment of risks, and appropriateness of the effort assigned to work packages, and the resources overall.
Capacity and role of each participant, and the extent to which the consortium as a whole brings together the necessary expertise.

grade_descriptors.0: The proposal fails to address the criterion or cannot be assessed due to missing or incomplete information.
grade_descriptors.1: Poor. The criterion is inadequately addressed, or there are serious inherent weaknesses.
grade_descriptors.2: Fair. The proposal broadly addresses the criterion, but there are significant weaknesses.
grade_descriptors.3: Good. The proposal addresses the criterion well, but a number of shortcomings are present.
grade_descriptors.4: Very Good. The proposal addresses the criterion very well, but a small number of shortcomings are present.
grade_descriptors.5: Excellent. The proposal successfully addresses all relevant aspects of the criterion. Any shortcomings are minor.

Quality and Efficiency of Implementation.call_specific_priority_notes: Standard RIA evaluation criterion. Call-specific implementation requirements include: mandatory allocation of tasks for cohesion activities with ADRA and the GenAI4EU central Hub; linkage to AI Factories and Data Labs; development of synergies with international, European, national, or regional initiatives; reuse of public results from previously funded actions where possible. Source: evaluation_priority_weights.json, criterion_id Quality and Efficiency of Implementation, priority_notes field.

overall_scoring_logic.weighting_note: Scores are normally NOT weighted per the evaluation form. Weighting is only used for some types of actions and only for ranking, not for threshold determination. Specific calls or topics may have different rules regarding thresholds and weighting.

compliance_checklist.description: Scope compliance: the proposal must correspond, wholly or in part, to the topic description against which it has been submitted. Evaluators assess whether the application is 'in scope' or 'out of scope'; if out of scope, a justification is required.
compliance_checklist.source_section: Section 2, Other Questions — Scope of the application

compliance_checklist.description: Exceptional funding for third country participants: a third country participant or international organisation not listed in the General Annex to the Main Work Programme may only receive funding if their participation is essential for carrying out the project (e.g., outstanding expertise, unique know-how, research infrastructure, geographical access, data). Per-participant justification must be provided and assessed by evaluators.

Use of human embryonic stem cells (hESC): the proposal must declare whether it involves the use of hESC. If yes, the evaluator must state whether hESC use is necessary to achieve the scientific objectives or declare if necessity cannot be assessed due to insufficient information.
compliance_checklist.source_section: Section 2, Other Questions — Use of human embryonic stem cells (hESC)

compliance_checklist.description: Use of human embryos: the proposal must declare whether it involves the use of human embryos and explain their planned use in the project if applicable.
compliance_checklist.source_section: Section 2, Other Questions — Use of human embryos

compliance_checklist.description: Activities excluded from funding: the proposal must not include activities that (a) aim at human cloning for reproductive purposes; (b) intend to modify the heritable genetic heritage of human beings (except cancer treatment of gonads); (c) intend to create human embryos solely for research or stem cell procurement including via somatic cell nuclear transfer; or (d) lead to the destruction of human embryos.
compliance_checklist.source_section: Section 2, Other Questions — Activities excluded from funding

compliance_checklist.description: Exclusive focus on civil applications: the proposed activities must have an exclusive focus on civil applications. Activities intended to be used in military applications or serving military purposes cannot be funded; non-compliance must be explained.
compliance_checklist.source_section: Section 2, Other Questions — Exclusive focus on civil applications

compliance_checklist.description: Lump sum budget assessment (conditional, applicable to lump sum topics only): for topics using lump sum funding, evaluators must assess cost estimations and confirm approval of the lump sum budget or include recommendations for modification in the evaluation report. Applicability to topic HORIZON-CL4-2026-05-DIGITAL-EMERGING-02 requires verification against call-specific provisions; standard RIA cost model may apply instead.
compliance_checklist.source_section: Section 2, Other Questions — Assessment of lump sum cost estimations (additional option for lump sum topics)

call_analysis_notes: Evaluation form used: ef_he-ria-ia_en.pdf — Horizon Europe Evaluation Form (HE RIA and IA), Version 4.0, dated 12 November 2025. This is the current English-language evaluation form directly matching the resolved_instrument_type 'RIA'; it was the sole file in the ria_ia/ subdirectory of docs/tier2a_instrument_schemas/evaluation_forms/. Three evaluation criteria were extracted from Section 1 (Evaluation) of the form: (1) Excellence — criterion 1, individual threshold 3/5; (2) Impact — criterion 2, individual threshold 3/5; (3) Quality and efficiency of the implementation — criterion 3, individual threshold 3/5. Overall maximum score: 15; overall threshold: 10. The evaluation form explicitly states that scores are normally NOT weighted; weighting is only used for some types of actions and only for ranking purposes, not for threshold determination. Specific calls or topics may have different weighting rules. Weights overlay from evaluation_priority_weights.json: all three Tier 2B entries carry weight: null, confirming that no explicit numerical weights are stated in the topic call extract for HORIZON-CL4-2026-05-DIGITAL-EMERGING-02. All evaluation_matrix criterion weight fields are accordingly null; no weight_source is asserted. The three criterion_ids in evaluation_priority_weights.json ('Excellence', 'Impact', 'Quality and Efficiency of Implementation') were successfully matched to the three criteria parsed from the evaluation form; no unmatched weight entries. Call-specific priority notes from the Tier 2B file have been carried into evaluation_matrix entries as call_specific_priority_notes to support downstream drafting and review phases. The compliance checklist (CR-01 to CR-07) was sourced exclusively from Section 2 (Other Questions) of the evaluation form; eligibility_conditions.json was not read as it is not in this skill's declared reads_from. The call is a single-stage call (deadline_model: single-stage per selected_call.json); two-stage evaluation rules stated in the form's Important Notice section are not applicable. CR-07 (lump sum budget assessment) is flagged as requires_review pending confirmation of whether this RIA topic uses lump sum funding.
